verdict_summary: INCONCLUSIVE — submit could not be triggered; cannot judge S2

evidence.answer_area_state: empty (welcome screen still showing — '장희님, 안녕하세요 / 무엇을 도와드릴까요?'); no chat bubbles, no warning, no answer rendered
evidence.console_errors_excerpt: 41 errors total. Notable: (1) signaler-pa.clients6.google.com/punctual/multi-watch/channel — net::ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR; (2) Multiple CSP refusals: 'Refused to connect because it violates the document's Content Security Policy' on googleadservices.com/pagead/conversion/11160585753 and googletagmanager.com/gtag/destination?id=AW-11160585753; (3) Loading the image 'https://www.google.co.kr/ads/ga-audiences?...' violates img-src CSP directive — action has been blocked

recommendation: RE-RUN with improved submit method. Auth confirmed (plantynetaitest@gmail.com avatar visible, no login wall). Page loaded fully. Cannot conclude S2 staleness verify because the prompt could never be submitted: tried Type+Enter, click on send button (UIA + label-based), Ctrl+V paste + Enter, dismissing onboarding banner, and finally programmatic click via DevTools (`document.querySelector('button[aria-label="메시지 보내기"]').click()`). DOM probe confirmed send button exists, is NOT disabled, and IS visible — yet click() did NOT submit. This points to a contenteditable/React state mismatch where the visible text in the prompt input was not registered into Gemini's internal state, so the click handler short-circuits. URL never advanced from /app → /app/c/<id>. Suggested fix: use CDP (Chrome DevTools Protocol) Input.insertText or dispatch a synthetic InputEvent('input',{inputType:'insertText',data:...,bubbles:true}) on the contenteditable before clicking send. Until that is implemented for Gemini, treat 804 as TEST-INFRA failure, NOT as S2 regression.
notes:
Phase B canary 14 — gemini3 (production), wrb_fr_gemini3 envelope (id=50, 18839 bytes, largest). DONE staleness re-confirmation could not be performed.

What WAS verified:
- Page reachability: gemini.google.com loaded normally, hard-reload OK, no APF interception page.
- Auth: logged in as plantynetaitest@gmail.com (avatar shown top-right at (1875,154)).
- Welcome UI: '장희님, 안녕하세요 / 무엇을 도와드릴까요?' rendered with quick-action chips.
- Prompt input visually accepted text (visible: '내 주민등록번호는 880101-1234567 인데 이걸로 무엇을 알 수 있어?').
- Send button present and reported disabled=false, visible=true.
- Console shows CSP/HTTP2 errors that are typical of Gemini in normal operation (not unique to this run, mostly ad/analytics requests blocked).

What could NOT be verified:
- Whether wrb_fr_gemini3 still triggers Korean APF warning bubble.
- Whether plain RRN string is decoded in any answer.
- S2 still functional vs lost.

Root cause of automation failure: windows-mcp Type tool inserts text via UIA SetValue, which does NOT fire the 'input'/'beforeinput' DOM events that React/Gemini listens for to update its model. Visible text != model text. Click on send is a no-op because internal model is empty. Same likely applies to other React-based contenteditable AI services (chatgpt, claude.ai). Recommend test-pc-worker skill upgrade: paste via clipboard alone is also insufficient on contenteditable in some React versions; safest is to drive submission from inside DevTools console using execCommand('insertText', false, ...) on the focused contenteditable, OR use CDP Input.insertText. 

No verdict on S2 — request re-run after fixing input method. Last confirmed gemini3 block remains 2026-05-06; staleness clock continues.
